Event: His father wants him to give up performing to be a doctor
Explanation: Father attempts to redirect Mike to a higher-status career, benefiting perceived family status while suppressing Mike's passion.

Event: His father disowns him for pursuing his dancing career
Explanation: Disowning Mike causes serious emotional/familial harm with no clear benefit to the father.
Father reconciling restores the relationship and supports Mike’s chosen path, helping both.

Event: When forced to choose between Glee Club and Football, he went with Glee Club without a second thought
Explanation: Choosing Glee nurtures his talents and identity while strengthening the club and its performances.

Event: Mike is on the academic decathlon team
Explanation: Participation builds his academic record and contributes skills/effort to the team.

Event: He became a series regular in the third season
Explanation: Promoting him increases his screen time/career value and enriches the show’s ensemble.
Vocal training improves his skills and enhances group performances.

Event: Mike and Matt maintain a close friendship (heterosexual life-partners)
Explanation: Their strong, supportive friendship benefits both and adds positive social support to others.

Event: Mike forms an easy friendship with the “losers” of the group
Explanation: He gains a solid friend group while offering acceptance and enthusiasm to marginalized peers.

Event: He's one of the most enthusiastic members of New Directions, always moving as soon as music starts
Explanation: His enthusiasm expresses his passion and energetically lifts group morale and performances.

Event: He commutes on a bus in season 4
Explanation: Neutral-mildly positive practical action that enables him to get where he needs to go without impacting others much.

Event: He hooks up with Tina after she sees him shirtless at Asian Camp
Explanation: Mutual attraction leads to a consensual hookup that appears to start a stable relationship benefiting both.

Event: Mike and Tina are in a stable relationship throughout seasons 2 and 3
Explanation: Long-term supportive relationship benefits both partners and contributes stability to their social circle.

Event: Mike and Tina break up shortly before the beginning of season 4
Explanation: Breakup ends a very stable, positive relationship with mutual emotional loss and no stated offsetting benefit.

Event: Mike has an offscreen breakup with Tina at the start of season 4
Explanation: A second framing of the same breakup emphasizes mutual loss without shown gain.

Event: Mike has an offscreen breakup with Brittany between late season 1 and season 2
Explanation: Ending a presumably lighter relationship brings some emotional cost and ambiguity with no depicted benefit.

Event: Mike and Tina start dating by season 2
Explanation: Forming a romantic relationship gives both emotional fulfillment and companionship.

Event: Mike and Brittany end their implied relationship before season 2 so he can date Tina
Explanation: Mike appears to move from Brittany to Tina, benefiting from a more compatible relationship while causing Brittany some loss.
